hero_subtitle: SFTY helps VASPs structure onboarding, Travel Rule routing, regulatory monitoring and case management into one unified ADGM compliance workflow with audit-ready evidence.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling

problem_title: Compliance slows when providers stay disconnected
problem_description: Digital asset compliance teams face inefficiencies when jurisdiction-specific data and provider outputs remain separated across multiple systems. This fragmentation creates delays in onboarding, weakens audit evidence and complicates defensible compliance decisions within the ADGM regulatory environment.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY brings together jurisdiction-specific provider data, onboarding checks, Travel Rule events and regulatory rules into one operational workflow tailored for VASPs in the ADGM compliance environment.
workflow_title: From regulatory change to internal rules
workflow_description: SFTY monitors ADGM regulatory updates, maps changes to internal policies, routes reviews, opens cases, records decisions and preserves evidence for compliance control.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects disparate provider outputs into a unified ADGM compliance layer, enabling structured workflows, case management, audit evidence, and regulatory rule alignment for VASPs.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY gives VASPs a controlled compliance layer supporting jurisdiction-specific workflows, evidence preservation and enterprise deployment for robust ADGM regulatory oversight.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for ADGM compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides ADGM compliance teams with a connected operating layer that connects onboarding, KYC, KYT, Travel Rule checks, cases, rules monitoring and reporting in one structured workflow.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support ADGM Travel Rule workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY supports ADGM Travel Rule by managing message routing, status tracking, exception handling, case creation and audit evidence within the wider compliance workflow.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready compliance evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, case histories and linked provider outputs as audit-ready evidence to support regulatory review and compliance traceability under ADGM rules.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing ADGM providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects outputs from existing ADGM-compliant KYC, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers without requiring replacement or disruption to your current stack.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace ADGM onboarding or KYT providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY operates above existing onboarding, KYC and KYT providers and structures their outputs into auditable compliance workflows without replacing the underlying services.
final_title: Structure ADGM compliance into connected workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY connects onboarding, Travel Rule routing, regulatory monitoring and case evidence into one controlled ADGM compliance layer.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.

footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
